title: AI Transformation & Operations Lead
summary: Embedded directly within the Claims organization, you will serve as the bridge between operational reality and technological capability. You will work in close partnership with Technology teams to extend the impact of existing tools and identify new opportunities that require technical development or integration. You will ensure that AI systems and our claims talent work seamlessly together—delivering measurable outcomes and building a more adaptive, data-driven operating model.
why_match: Role focuses on bridging operational reality with technological capability, indicating some element of transformation
Involves partnership with Technology teams to extend tool impact and identify new technical opportunities
Role is remote, which aligns with preference for remote jobs
concerns: Position is embedded directly within the Claims organization, a single business function, limiting enterprise-wide impact
Emphasis on AI systems working with claims talent suggests a narrower focus rather than broad platform transformation or modernization
No explicit indication of enterprise-scale platform transformation, governance, or operating model redesign across multiple teams or systems

title: Product Manager, Commerce Systems
why_match: Role focuses on harmonizing and unifying complex internal billing, seller systems, and financial data warehouses into a single platform, aligning well with enterprise platform transformation and system consolidation.

Involves leadership in designing infrastructure control planes that serve multiple disparate stakeholders (internal product teams, finance, legal), which maps to building and modernizing platform infrastructure at scale.
Remote full-time role with a strong emphasis on backend systems and API-first mindset, aligning well with candidate’s preference for remote product management roles focused on platform modernization.
Role requires driving strategy and navigating complexity across multiple teams and systems, which ties into operating model redesign and enterprise-wide system modernization.
concerns: Role is heavily internal and focused on Stripe’s product teams rather than traditional external enterprise customers, though this is typical in strong platform PM roles at product companies.

title: AI Delivery Lead
summary: HMH’s Content Solutions Team is accelerating the integration of AI and automation across content development to improve speed, quality, consistency, and cost efficiency. The AI Delivery Lead coordinates and manages the adoption, utilization, and operational integration of AI/automation capabilities across NWEA’s products, state programs, and formative solutions. This role leads the organization and governance of AI initiatives, coordinates pilots, evaluates new capabilities, and develops SOPs/ guidelines. Additionally, this role supports the communication, training, and adoption of these initiatives across the division. The AI Delivery Lead also aligns AI/automation plans with capacity, sequencing, and agile practices to ensure predictable delivery and measurable impact.
why_match: Role involves coordinating and managing adoption and operational integration of AI/automation capabilities across multiple products and programs, suggesting enterprise-wide impact
Focus on governance, organization, SOPs, and guidelines aligns with operating model redesign responsibilities
Role supports communication, training, and adoption across division, implying cross-team influence and transformation
Remote role
concerns: No explicit mention of platform modernization or system consolidation
Role appears focused specifically on AI Delivery and adoption rather than broader enterprise platform transformation
Potentially more program coordination than technical platform transformation leadership

title: Credit Risk Program Manager, Risk Foundations

Responsibilities include designing and implementing scalable risk-wide frameworks and foundational policies, indicating involvement in governance and operating model redesign at scale.

concerns: The role focuses primarily on credit risk management and associated processes, which may be more specialized and embedded within a specific business function (Risk) rather than broad enterprise platform transformation.
